_doc:
Phase 8 demo — the hand-written Verilog parser in action.

This circuit is a small 2:1 MUX with a registered output. The corresponding hand-written Verilog source lives at `examples/hdl-corpus/phase8-mux2-handwritten.v`. To compare:
  • Click VERILOG in the bottom toolbar → see what our exporter produces from THIS scene.
  • Open the sidecar `.v` (Phase 8 import path) — same 2:1 MUX written by hand, exercising parameter, attribute, case, async-reset always, and $display.
  • The parser tests (`test-hdl-parser*.mjs`) round-trip both: the exporter's output and the hand-written sidecar. Either text → AST → re-print → AST is structurally identical.

What Phase 8 ships:
  • Lexer: identifiers, sized & unsized numbers (`8'hFF`, `4'b10x1`), operators, keywords, line/block comments, `(* attribute *)` blocks with line/col tracking. System identifiers (`$display`, `$readmemh`).
  • AST: 27 node kinds covering modules, ports, nets, memories, assigns, always/initial, primitive + module instances, all expression forms (literal, ref, slice, index, binary/unary/ternary, concat, replicate, paren, system call, string literal), parameters.
  • Parser: recursive-descent + Pratt precedence climber. Error recovery at statement boundaries — collects multiple errors in one pass instead of stopping on the first. Resource limits (max recursion depth, max tokens, parse deadline) protect against adversarial input.
  • L1 gate: every Phase-1..7 demo's exported `.v` parses cleanly AND round-trips through AST → re-print → AST with structural equality.
